title: Pit Firing: Outdoor Basics
notes: Using an outdoor pit (30-40 cm deep) or a galvanised metal dustbin as a simple saggar. Earthenware or raku clay packed with sawdust, newspaper, and wood shavings and fired in situ. Reaches approximately 600-900 degrees C - lower than a formal bisque but sufficient to harden earthenware to a decorative finish. Produces atmospheric surface marks from combustible smoke. No commercial kiln required. Covers pit preparation, loading, ignition, burn management, and safe extraction. Mandatory fire safety guidance.
